content: We can frame a curiosity-driven autonomous agent as a triple-signal hybrid retrieval system that continuously pulls three distinct signals from its environment to update its memory: intrinsic reward prediction error as a relevance signal, surprise magnitude as a novelty signal, and long-term goal alignment as a priority signal. This re-framing enables the agent to dynamically rank and store only the most useful new experiences, avoiding catastrophic forgetting and reducing memory bloat common in large active agent systems.
